au_summary: Comprehensive registration records across all Australian jurisdictions including status, operator, vehicle class, and expiry.
au_detail: NEVDIS holds real-time registration data from all 8 Australian states and territories. Records include VIN/chassis number, make, model, body type, vehicle type, year of manufacture, engine capacity, motive power, tare weight, colour, jurisdiction, registration ID, plate number, registration status, and registration dates. Data is updated in real-time from state and territory authority feeds. Used for ownership verification, fraud detection, VIN validation, compliance checking, registration renewal, insurance assessment, and fleet management. Key fields: VIN/Chassis Number (String), Make (String), Model (String), Body Type (String), Vehicle Type (String), Year of Manufacture (Number), Engine Capacity (Number), Motive Power (String), Tare Weight (Number), Colour (String), Jurisdiction (String), Registration ID (String), Plate Number (String), Status (String), Registration Start (Date), Registration Expiry (Date).
au_access_info: Contact nevdis@austroads.com.au. Requires jurisdictional eligibility, demonstrated legitimate use case, and a formal data sharing agreement. Access is not available to individuals — organisations must apply.
au_keywords: registration, vehicle, plate, status, operator, jurisdiction, expiry, fleet, compliance
au_delivery_method: Real-time data feeds to authorised organisations
au_process_steps:
1. State/territory road agencies update registration records in their systems.
2. Changes are pushed to NEVDIS in real-time via jurisdictional data feeds.
3. NEVDIS validates and stores records in the national database.
4. Authorised organisations query NEVDIS for current registration data.

au_summary: The authoritative repository of 17-character VINs for all vehicles manufactured from 1989 onwards, compliant with ISO 3780 and ADR 61/2.
au_detail: Central repository of 17-character Vehicle Identification Numbers (VINs) compliant with ADR 61/2 and ISO 3780. The VIN consists of three sections: World Manufacturer Identifier (WMI, 3 chars), Vehicle Descriptor Section (VDS, 6 chars), and Vehicle Identifier Section (VIS, 8 chars) including a check digit. Covers all vehicles manufactured from 1989 onwards, both local and imported. Manufacturers submit VINs pre-registration, NEVDIS validates them, passes to ROVER, and state/territory registration authorities access the data. WMI Application costs $350 ex GST, VIN Submitter Accreditation costs $350 ex GST. Key fields: VIN (String, 17 chars), World Manufacturer Identifier/WMI (String, 3 chars), Vehicle Descriptor Section/VDS (String, 6 chars), Vehicle Identifier Section/VIS (String, 8 chars), Check Digit (String, 1 char). Accreditation fees: WMI Application $350 ex GST, VIN Submitter Accreditation $350 ex GST.
au_access_info: Contact nevdis@austroads.com.au for VIN submission or access enquiries.
au_keywords: VIN, vehicle identification number, chassis, manufacturer, ADR, ISO 3780, WMI, ROVER
au_delivery_method: VIN submission and validation via NEVDIS
au_process_steps:
1. Manufacturer applies for WMI and VIN Submitter accreditation.
2. Manufacturer submits VINs to NEVDIS prior to vehicle registration.
3. NEVDIS validates VIN structure (ISO 3780, ADR 61/2) and stores in national database.
4. VIN passed to ROVER and made available to state/territory registration authorities.

au_summary: Authoritative driver licence records from all Australian jurisdictions including status, class, conditions, and demerit points.
au_detail: NEVDIS holds driver licence records from all 8 Australian states and territories. Data includes licence number, given name, surname, date of birth, address, gender, jurisdiction, licence class, status, expiry date, demerit points, and conditions. Used for licence verification, fraud prevention, cross-jurisdictional compliance, electoral roll updating (AEC integration), and roadside checking. All records are covered by the Privacy Act 1988 and access is restricted to authorised organisations only. Key fields: Licence Number (String), Given Name (String), Surname (String), Date of Birth (Date), Address (String), Gender (String), Jurisdiction (String), Licence Class (String), Status (String), Expiry Date (Date), Demerit Points (Number), Conditions (String).
au_access_info: Contact nevdis@austroads.com.au. Requires jurisdictional eligibility and formal data sharing agreement. Privacy Act 1988 applies.
au_keywords: driver, licence, license, demerit, conditions, identity, verification, cross-border
au_delivery_method: Real-time data feeds to authorised organisations
au_process_steps:
1. State/territory licensing authorities issue or update driver licence records.
2. Changes are pushed to NEVDIS in real-time via jurisdictional data feeds.
3. NEVDIS validates and stores records in the national database.
4. Authorised organisations query NEVDIS for current licence data (Privacy Act 1988 applies).

au_summary: National records of stolen vehicles and written-off vehicle restrictions from police and state registries.
au_detail: NEVDIS maintains national records of stolen vehicles and Written-Off Vehicle Register (WOVR) entries. Data includes VIN/chassis, plate number, report date, reporting jurisdiction, status (STOLEN/RECOVERED), write-off type (SWO — Statutory Write-Off, RWO — Repairable Write-Off), write-off date, and re-registration restrictions. Used for pre-purchase checks, dealer compliance, insurance assessment, fraud detection, roadside enforcement, and consumer protection. Updated in real-time from police and state registry feeds across all 8 jurisdictions. Key fields (Stolen): VIN/Chassis (String), Plate Number (String), Report Date (Date), Reporting Jurisdiction (String), Status (String: STOLEN/RECOVERED). Key fields (Write-off): Write-off Type (String: SWO/RWO), Write-off Date (Date), Reporting Jurisdiction (String), Re-registration Restriction (Boolean).
au_access_info: Contact nevdis@austroads.com.au. Access available to authorised organisations including police, insurers, dealers, and consumer protection bodies.
au_keywords: stolen, write-off, WOVR, SWOVS, repairable, statutory, fraud, consumer protection
au_delivery_method: Real-time data feeds to authorised organisations
au_process_steps:
1. Police report a vehicle as stolen or record a write-off via their systems.
2. Record is pushed to NEVDIS in real-time from police and state registries.
3. NEVDIS stores the record and flags the VIN nationally.
4. Authorised organisations query NEVDIS for stolen/write-off status checks.

au_summary: Manufacturer-supplied vehicle specifications from FCAI/VFACTS — the single source of truth for vehicle identification and ADR compliance matching.
au_detail: Premium enriched vehicle dataset combining registration data with manufacturer specifications sourced from the Federal Chamber of Automotive Industries (FCAI) via VFACTS (now operated by S&P Global). Includes make, model, series/variant, body type, engine code, engine capacity (cc), fuel type, transmission, ADR compliance month, and GVM (kg). Used for registration validation against manufacturer records, specification lookups, ADR compliance checking, VIN cross-referencing, fleet analytics, and regulatory reporting. Key fields: Make (String), Model (String), Series/Variant (String), Body Type (String), Engine Code (String), Engine Capacity cc (Number), Fuel Type (String), Transmission (String), ADR Compliance Month (String), GVM kg (Number).
au_access_info: Contact nevdis@austroads.com.au for access enquiries.
au_keywords: gold, FCAI, VFACTS, manufacturer, specifications, make, model, engine, GVM, ADR
au_data_source: FCAI (Federal Chamber of Automotive Industries), VFACTS (S&P Global)
au_process_steps:
1. Manufacturers submit vehicle specifications to FCAI/VFACTS.
2. FCAI/VFACTS (operated by S&P Global) publishes specification data.
3. NEVDIS ingests and maps specifications to VIN records.
4. Authorised organisations access enriched vehicle data via NEVDIS queries.

au_summary: Secure API service converting an Australian registration plate and state into a VIN or chassis number for authorised commercial brokers.
au_detail: Plate-to-VIN (P2V) is a secure REST API service that converts a registration plate number and state/territory into a VIN (17-character for vehicles 1989+) or chassis number (pre-1989 vehicles). Input: plate number, state/territory, enquiry message. Output: VIN or chassis number. Delivered as a real-time JSON/XML API response. Used for fleet vehicle identification, insurance verification, vehicle identity confirmation, and plate-to-VIN cross-referencing. Currently restricted to 5 authorised brokers: VEHICLEID, EVOLVE, BLUEFLAG, INFOAGENT, and CARSALES. Inputs: Plate Number (String, required), State/Territory (String, required), Enquiry Message (String, required). Outputs: VIN (String, 17 chars), Chassis Number (String, pre-1989 vehicles).
au_access_info: Currently restricted to 5 approved brokers. New broker applications are NOT currently being accepted due to resource constraints. Contact nevdis@austroads.com.au for enquiries.
au_keywords: P2V, plate to VIN, plate, API, broker, VEHICLEID, EVOLVE, BLUEFLAG, INFOAGENT, CARSALES
au_delivery_method: REST API (JSON or XML), Real-time response
au_process_steps:
1. Authorised broker submits plate number, state/territory, and enquiry message via REST API.
2. NEVDIS queries the registration database for the matching plate.
3. VIN (17-char for 1989+ vehicles) or chassis number (pre-1989) is retrieved.
4. Result returned to broker as real-time JSON or XML response.

au_summary: Real-time lookup matching ACCC manufacturer safety recall notices against registered VINs for consumer protection and compliance.
au_detail: Real-time lookup service matching ACCC-registered manufacturer safety recall notices against VINs in NEVDIS. Input: VIN (17-character). Output: recall status (boolean), recall reference (ACCC number), recall description, manufacturer contact. Data source: ACCC recall notices combined with manufacturer-supplied VIN lists. Recalls are added in real-time as the ACCC publishes them. Used for pre-purchase safety verification, dealer pre-delivery inspection, fleet recall management, ACCC compliance checking, and roadside enforcement. Inputs: VIN (String, 17-char, required). Outputs: Recall Status (Boolean), Recall Reference (String, ACCC number), Recall Description (String), Manufacturer Contact (String).
au_access_info: Contact nevdis@austroads.com.au for API access.
au_keywords: recall, safety, ACCC, manufacturer, VIN, consumer protection, pre-purchase
au_data_source: ACCC recall notices, Manufacturer-supplied VIN lists
au_delivery_method: API query by VIN, Real-time response
au_process_steps:
1. Manufacturer lodges a recall notice with ACCC and NEVDIS, including a list of affected VINs.
2. NEVDIS stores the recall notice and associates it with affected VINs in the national database.
3. User or system queries NEVDIS with a VIN to check recall status.
4. NEVDIS returns current recall status matched against active recall records in real time.

au_summary: Daily scheduled extract of driver licence holder changes supplied exclusively to the Australian Electoral Commission for electoral roll accuracy.
au_detail: Daily scheduled extract of driver licence holder record changes supplied exclusively to the Australian Electoral Commission (AEC). Data elements include name, address, DOB, and citizenship status (where available). Process: NEVDIS identifies daily changes, generates a scheduled extract, transmits securely to the AEC, and the AEC updates the electoral roll. Supports automatic electoral roll updates including address changes, new enrolments, cancellations, and deaths. Governed by formal data sharing agreement, Privacy Act 1988, and Commonwealth Electoral Act. Key fields in extract: Name (String), Address (String), Date of Birth (Date), Citizenship Status (String).
au_access_info: AEC only — access is under a formal data sharing agreement. Not available to other organisations.
au_keywords: AEC, electoral, commission, roll, daily, extract, enrolment, address
au_delivery_method: Daily scheduled extract, Secure file transfer
au_process_steps:
1. NEVDIS identifies all driver licence holder records changed in the previous 24 hours.
2. Scheduled daily extract generated with relevant holder details (name, address, DOB, citizenship status).
3. Extract transmitted securely to AEC via agreed data sharing channels.
4. AEC processes extract to update electoral roll, matching against existing enrolment records.

au_summary: Aggregated, de-identified vehicle registration data supplied annually to BITRE for Australia's official Motor Vehicle Census publication.
au_detail: Annual aggregated extract of vehicle registration data supplied to the Bureau of Infrastructure and Transport Research Economics (BITRE). Process: annual snapshot of registered vehicles is taken, aggregated and de-identified, then BITRE produces the official Motor Vehicle Census publication. Used for fleet composition analysis, emissions estimation, infrastructure planning, and government/research/industry fleet analysis. Delivery: annual aggregated extract at a census point-in-time snapshot. Key fields: Aggregated Vehicle Counts (Number), Vehicle Type Breakdown (String), Jurisdiction Summary (String).
au_access_info: BITRE only — supplied under formal arrangement. The published census data is publicly available from BITRE.
au_keywords: BITRE, census, annual, aggregated, fleet, emissions, infrastructure, statistics
au_data_source: NEVDIS registration snapshot across all jurisdictions
au_process_steps:
1. At agreed annual census date, NEVDIS extracts snapshot of registered vehicle records across all jurisdictions.
2. Records aggregated and de-identified (no individual owner information).
3. BITRE receives aggregated dataset and produces Motor Vehicle Census publication.
4. Motor Vehicle Census published publicly by BITRE.

au_summary: Validates VINs against the Register of Approved Vehicles and shares approval data with state/territory registries for ADR-compliant vehicle registration.
au_detail: NEVDIS data supporting the Register of Approved Vehicles (RAV) programme. Validates VINs against RAV and shares approval data with state/territory registration authorities. Used for vehicle registration compliance, approval validation, and ensuring ADR compliance for vehicles supplied in Australia. Consumers include state/territory registration authorities, vehicle importers, and DITRDCA. Provides real-time VIN validation against the register. Inputs: VIN (String, required). Outputs: RAV Approval Status (Boolean), Vehicle Model Approval (String).
au_access_info: Contact nevdis@austroads.com.au for integration enquiries.
au_keywords: RAV, register, approved, vehicles, ADR, compliance, importers, DITRDCA
au_process_steps:
1. DITRDCA approves vehicle model for supply in Australia and records on RAV.
2. NEVDIS receives RAV approval data and associates with relevant VINs.
3. When jurisdiction attempts to register vehicle, NEVDIS checks VIN against RAV.
4. Registration proceeds only if VIN associated with RAV-approved vehicle.

au_summary: Core registration and identity data for heavy vehicles enrolled in the National Telematics Framework, linking vehicles, devices, operators, and jurisdictions.
au_detail: Data from TCA's Enrolment Management System (EMS) covering heavy vehicles enrolled in the National Telematics Framework (NTF). Key data includes vehicle VIN, registration plate, vehicle class, telematics device ID, service provider, operator name, enrolment status (Active/Suspended/Cancelled), and jurisdiction. Used for vehicle identity verification for compliance, linking telematics data to vehicles, operator accreditation checking, and jurisdictional oversight. Updated in real-time on enrolment change events. Governed by National Telematics Framework and jurisdiction legislation. Key fields: Vehicle VIN (String), Registration Plate (String), Vehicle Class (String), Telematics Device ID (String), Service Provider (String), Operator Name (String), Enrolment Status (String: Active/Suspended/Cancelled), Jurisdiction (String).
au_access_info: Submit access request via urm.tca.gov.au (TAP User Enrolment portal). Approval takes approximately 2 business days. Contact support@tca.gov.au for enquiries.
au_keywords: EMS, enrolment, NTF, telematics, heavy vehicle, operator, device, service provider
au_data_source: NTF enrolment events from telematics service providers
au_delivery_method: Secure data access via TCA systems
au_process_steps:
1. Heavy vehicle operator enrols vehicle in the National Telematics Framework via an accredited service provider.
2. TCA records enrolment linking vehicle, device, operator, and jurisdiction.
3. Enrolment data is updated in real-time on change events (activation, suspension, cancellation).
4. Authorised parties access enrolment data via TCA data systems.

au_summary: GNSS telemetry capturing vehicle movement, speed, and positional accuracy at trip level — the raw data underpinning all TCA products.
au_detail: GNSS telemetry data from NTF-enrolled heavy vehicles capturing position, speed, heading, and accuracy at regular intervals. Key data: timestamp (UTC), latitude (WGS84), longitude (WGS84), speed (km/h), heading (degrees), HDOP (accuracy), trip ID, device ID. Used for route compliance checking (IAC/NHVR permits), speed monitoring, trip reconstruction, road usage analysis, freight network analytics, and infrastructure planning. Coordinate system: WGS84 (decimal degrees). Continuous real-time telemetry stream. Privacy obligations apply. Key fields: Timestamp (DateTime, UTC), Latitude (Float, WGS84), Longitude (Float, WGS84), Speed km/h (Number), Heading degrees (Number), HDOP (Float), Trip ID (String), Device ID (String).
au_access_info: Submit access request via urm.tca.gov.au. Governed by TAP Terms and Conditions. Contact support@tca.gov.au for enquiries.
au_keywords: GNSS, GPS, telemetry, position, speed, heading, trip, movement, real-time, telematics
au_data_source: GNSS telematics devices on NTF-enrolled heavy vehicles
au_process_steps:
1. GNSS telematics device on enrolled heavy vehicle records position at regular intervals.
2. Records streamed in real-time to TCA via accredited service providers.
3. TCA stores and validates positional records with trip-level grouping.
4. Authorised users access spatiotemporal data via secure API or extracts.

au_summary: Standardised spatial datasets from Geoscape layers defining road network access rules and constraints for IAC and B2B systems.
au_detail: Standardised spatial datasets derived from Geoscape PSMA road network layers combined with jurisdiction access rules and bridge data. Defines IAC road network (approved routes with vehicle class conditions), B2B spatial layer (bridge constraints, load limits, height restrictions), jurisdiction boundaries, and road segment access rules (time-of-day, vehicle class restrictions). Used for route compliance checking, operator route planning, telematics system integration, and road network analysis. Delivered via spatial API or GIS datasets on request. Geoscape licensing terms apply — redistribution requires TCA authorisation. Key datasets: IAC Road Network (approved routes by vehicle class), B2B Spatial Layer (bridge constraints, load/height limits), Jurisdiction Boundaries (state/territory layers), Road Segment Access Rules (time-of-day and vehicle class restrictions).
au_access_info: Submit access request via urm.tca.gov.au. Geoscape licensing terms apply. Contact support@tca.gov.au for enquiries.
au_keywords: spatial, IAC, B2B, Geoscape, road network, bridge, route, access rules, GIS
au_data_source: Geoscape PSMA road network, Jurisdiction access rules
au_delivery_method: Spatial API, GIS datasets on request
au_process_steps:
1. Spatial layers derived from Geoscape PSMA road network data.
2. Augmented with jurisdiction-supplied access rules, bridge data, and road condition information.
3. TCA standardises and validates layers before distribution.
4. Distributed via Spatial API or GIS datasets on request to authorised users.

au_summary: Aggregated analysis of heavy vehicle road usage across states, delivered via ArcGIS dashboards or bulk data extracts.
au_detail: Aggregated analysis of heavy vehicle road usage by corridor, state, and time period. Outputs include corridor usage counts (vehicle passes per segment/period), average speeds by time of day, and state-level summaries. Delivered via ArcGIS Enterprise interactive dashboards or bulk datasets on request. Updated monthly with aggregated data. Used by road authorities, infrastructure planners, state/territory transport agencies, freight policy developers, and research organisations. All data is aggregated and anonymised. Key outputs: Corridor Usage Counts (Number), Average Speeds (Number), State-level Summaries (Dataset), ArcGIS Dashboard (Interactive Map).
au_access_info: Submit access request via urm.tca.gov.au (TAP User Enrolment). Approval takes approximately 2 business days. Governed by TAP Terms and Conditions. Contact support@tca.gov.au.
au_keywords: road analytics, corridor, usage, ArcGIS, dashboard, infrastructure, planning, aggregated
au_data_source: Spatiotemporal telematics data from NTF-enrolled heavy vehicles
au_delivery_method: ArcGIS Enterprise dashboards, Bulk datasets on request
au_process_steps:
1. Spatiotemporal records from enrolled heavy vehicles are map-matched to road network segments.
2. Matched records are aggregated by road segment, time period, and vehicle class.
3. Aggregated metrics (vehicle counts, speeds, usage frequencies) are computed per corridor.
4. Results published to ArcGIS dashboards and available as bulk dataset extracts.

au_summary: Statistical analysis of Smart On-Board Mass data for NTF-enrolled vehicles — throughput and mass insights for infrastructure safety and compliance.
au_detail: Statistical analysis of Smart On-Board Mass (OBM) data from NTF-enrolled heavy vehicles. Outputs include load profiles, mass statistics by corridor, axle-level distribution, and pavement impact analysis. Smart OBM devices measure mass, which is combined with spatiotemporal position data, then aggregated by corridor and vehicle class. Delivered via ArcGIS Enterprise dashboards and statistical reports. Updated periodically as data volumes permit. Used by road authorities (pavement impact), heavy vehicle regulators, infrastructure planners, and researchers. Not for enforcement use. All data is aggregated and anonymised. Key outputs: Load Profiles (Dataset), Mass Statistics by Corridor (Number), Axle-level Distribution (Dataset), Pavement Impact Analysis (Dataset).
au_access_info: Submit access request via urm.tca.gov.au. Governed by TAP Terms and Conditions. Contact support@tca.gov.au.
au_keywords: OBM, on-board mass, weight, axle, pavement, load, mass compliance, Smart OBM
au_data_source: Smart OBM devices on NTF-enrolled heavy vehicles, combined with spatiotemporal position data
au_process_steps:
1. Smart OBM devices measure vehicle mass at the axle level in real time.
2. Mass readings combined with spatiotemporal position data to link loads to specific road segments.
3. Data aggregated by corridor, time period, and vehicle class to produce statistical summaries.
4. Outputs delivered via ArcGIS dashboards and analytical reports.

au_summary: Telematics-derived insights into approved high-productivity freight vehicle routes — B-double and road train analysis supporting route management.
au_detail: Telematics-derived insights into use of approved high-productivity freight vehicle routes. Covers B-doubles, road trains, and PBS vehicles. Outputs include annual usage counts, productivity scores, route metrics, and state-by-state breakdowns. Delivered via ArcGIS dashboard (annual) or bulk annual datasets on request. Updated annually with the previous year's aggregated data. Used for route prioritisation for upgrades, annual freight network reporting, new route approval evidence, and route productivity/utilisation assessment. All data is aggregated and anonymised, relative to monitored vehicles only. Key outputs: Annual Usage Counts (Number), Productivity Scores (Number), Route Metrics (Dataset), State-by-state Breakdowns (Dataset).
au_access_info: Submit access request via urm.tca.gov.au (TAP User Enrolment). Approval takes approximately 2 business days. Governed by TAP Terms and Conditions. Contact support@tca.gov.au.
au_keywords: HPFR, freight, routes, B-double, road train, PBS, productivity, annual
au_data_source: Telematics data from B-double, road train, and high-productivity freight vehicles
au_delivery_method: ArcGIS dashboard (annual), Annual bulk datasets on request
au_process_steps:
1. Telematics records from high-productivity vehicles filtered and matched to approved HPFR routes.
2. Annual aggregation produces usage counts, productivity scores, and route metrics.
3. Results published to HPFR ArcGIS dashboard with annual state-by-state breakdowns.
4. Bulk annual datasets available to authorised users on request.

au_summary: Aggregated insights into historical heavy vehicle movements across the NZ road network from a collaborative NRC/NZTA/Austroads study.
au_detail: A completed historical heavy vehicle movement study across the New Zealand road network, conducted in collaboration with NRC, NZTA, and Austroads. Covers approximately 120 Restricted Access Vehicles (RAV) with 30-second polling records, using NZTA AMDS road-segment geometry. Outputs include road usage heatmap, segment detail pop-ups, monthly usage charts, and regional/TA overlays. Covers Auckland, Waikato, and Bay of Plenty corridors. Delivered via ArcGIS Enterprise dashboard or bulk data on request. IMPORTANT: This is a static historical study only — it is NOT a live or ongoing operational service. Inputs: 30-second Polling Records (Dataset), NZTA AMDS Road-segment Geometry (Dataset), Year/Month Selection (String). Outputs: Road Usage Heatmap (Dataset), Segment Detail Pop-ups (Dataset), Monthly Usage Charts (Dataset), Regional/TA Overlays (Dataset).
au_access_info: Submit request via urm.tca.gov.au. Governed by TAP Terms and Conditions. Contact support@tca.gov.au.
au_keywords: New Zealand, NZ, NRC, NZTA, historical, RAV, heavy vehicle, study
au_data_source: 30-second polling records from ~120 RAVs, NZTA AMDS road-segment geometry
au_delivery_method: ArcGIS Enterprise dashboard, Bulk data on request
au_process_steps:
1. ~120 Restricted Access Vehicles tracked with 30-second GNSS polling records.
2. Records map-matched to NZTA AMDS road-segment geometry.
3. Aggregated by corridor and time period to produce usage heatmaps and charts.
4. Published via ArcGIS Enterprise dashboard for Auckland, Waikato, and Bay of Plenty corridors.

au_summary: Austroads is Australia's peak body for road transport and traffic management.
au_detail: Austroads is the peak organisation of Australasian road transport and traffic agencies. The Data Info Hub provides authorised users with information about data products and services available through two national programmes: NEVDIS (National Exchange of Vehicle and Driver Information System) for vehicle, registration, and driver licence data, and TCA (Transport Certification Australia) for heavy vehicle telematics and road usage data. The hub helps users understand what data is available, how to access it, and who to contact.
au_access_info: Visit the Data Info Hub at the site home page to explore NEVDIS and TCA data products.
au_keywords: Austroads, about, overview, hub, data, programmes

au_summary: NEVDIS is the National Exchange of Vehicle and Driver Information System — Australia's authoritative source for vehicle registration and driver licence data.
au_detail: NEVDIS (National Exchange of Vehicle and Driver Information System) is the authoritative national system for vehicle, registration, and driver licence data across all 8 Australian states and territories. It provides real-time data feeds to authorised organisations. NEVDIS offers 10 data products covering vehicle registrations, VINs, driver licences, stolen vehicle records, manufacturer specifications, plate-to-VIN lookups, safety recalls, electoral data provisioning, census data, and RAV validation.
au_access_info: Contact nevdis@austroads.com.au for access requests. Requires jurisdictional eligibility, demonstrated legitimate use case, and formal data sharing agreement.
au_keywords: NEVDIS, what is, overview, vehicle, driver, national

au_summary: TCA (Transport Certification Australia) operates the National Telematics Framework for heavy vehicle data and road usage analytics.
au_detail: TCA (Transport Certification Australia) operates data products and services built on the National Telematics Framework (NTF). TCA covers heavy vehicle location, speed, weight, and road access data across all Australian jurisdictions. TCA offers 7 data products covering vehicle enrolment, spatiotemporal telemetry, spatial road network data, road usage analytics, on-board mass monitoring, freight route analysis, and a New Zealand historical study.
au_access_info: Submit access request via urm.tca.gov.au (TAP User Enrolment portal). Contact support@tca.gov.au for enquiries.
au_keywords: TCA, what is, overview, telematics, heavy vehicle, NTF, transport

au_summary: How to get access to NEVDIS and TCA data products, including contact details and requirements.
au_detail: NEVDIS access: Contact nevdis@austroads.com.au. Requirements include jurisdictional eligibility, a demonstrated legitimate use case, and a formal data sharing agreement. Access is not available to individuals — organisations must apply. TCA access: Submit request via urm.tca.gov.au (TAP User Enrolment portal). Requires eligibility assessment, formal agreement, and onboarding to the National Telematics Framework. Approval takes approximately 2 business days. P2V (Plate-to-VIN) access: Currently restricted to 5 approved brokers (VEHICLEID, EVOLVE, BLUEFLAG, INFOAGENT, CARSALES). No new broker applications are being accepted at this time.
au_access_info: NEVDIS: nevdis@austroads.com.au | TCA: urm.tca.gov.au / support@tca.gov.au
au_keywords: access, contact, how to, apply, requirements, eligibility, agreement

au_summary: The Data Info Hub is a central catalogue for exploring and understanding Austroads data products across NEVDIS and TCA.
au_detail: The Austroads Data Info Hub is a central catalogue where authorised internal users can explore and understand all data products available through Austroads. It covers two programmes: NEVDIS (10 data products for vehicle/driver data) and TCA (7 data products for heavy vehicle telematics). Each product page describes what the data contains, how it is delivered, how often it is updated, who uses it, and how to request access. The AI assistant can help you navigate products, compare options, and find the right contact for access requests.
au_access_info: Browse the NEVDIS and TCA sections on the hub, or ask the AI assistant for help finding the right product.
au_keywords: hub, catalogue, how, navigate, explore, products, help
